method_atom: empirical characterization of the fluctuations of coarse-grained additive quantities measured over many finite windows of a signal, showing that these window-to-window fluctuations follow a lognormal distribution whenever the window size falls in an intermediate range where correlations are weak but nonzero, with the lognormality attributed to an underlying multiplicative stochastic process generated by hierarchical interactions that transfer a conserved quantity across scales
object_atom: velocity-field records from several laboratory turbulent flows, from which the mean energy-dissipation rate, the mean energy at each scale, and the mean total energy are computed segment by segment across Reynolds numbers and production configurations
brief_reason: the transferable piece is the multiplicative-cascade mechanism yielding universal lognormal statistics of coarse-grained observables at intermediate window sizes, while the turbulent velocity field and its energy quantities are the fluid-dynamics-specific object.
